disk_measurements.morphology: first sub-mm detection of cold Kuiper-analog ring; bolometer map at 850 micron with ~14 arcsec beam resolved ring-like structure at ~60 AU
warm inner asteroid-belt analog at ~3 AU from Spitzer/IRS 2-component SED decomposition; canonical inner-belt source. Model belt T ~120 K (observed range 100-150 K)

cold component of Spitzer/IRS+MIPS 2-component SED fit, color temperature ~55 K (audit 2026-05-29: was 35 K, which is the Greaves 1998 SCUBA value); SED-only, geometry refined later by MacGregor 2015 ALMA
narrow eccentric (e~0.07) cold ring resolved at 1.3 mm; ring center 64.4+/-0.5 AU recorded as inner_radius_au (narrow ring, no separate outer edge); canonical cold-ring source
inner warm component in Booth 2017 three-belt decomposition; SED-derived, geometry from Backman 2009
intermediate dust population at ~20 AU from Herschel SED decomposition (Greaves 2014); re-attributed 2026-05-29 from Booth 2017, which resolves only the 69 AU ring and finds no strong 20 AU emission
Northern arc of cold ring imaged with ALMA; refines geometry + inclination 34+/-2 deg; integrated three-belt dust mass ~0.04 M_earth
inner warm belt in Su 2017 Genie three-belt sculpting model; geometry inherited from Backman 2009, refined by Spitzer/MIPS imaging within 25 AU
intermediate belt in Su 2017 Genie model; required by SED decomposition between asteroid-analog and Kuiper-analog rings; canonical three-belt synthesis source
cold ring in Su 2017 Genie three-belt sculpting model; geometry from MacGregor 2015 ALMA, mass from Booth 2017; planetary sculptors inferred
narrow eccentric ring (e=0.11) with sharp inner edge
disk_measurements.notes: HST/ACS coronagraphy first resolved the main ring; eccentricity e~0.11; sharp inner edge interpreted as gravitational sculpting by an unseen shepherd body

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring, width Delta_a/a~0.13, e=0.11+/-0.01
disk_measurements.notes: ALMA Cycle 0 millimeter imaging; constrains shepherd planet mass to < 3 M_Jup; refines geometry of the main ring

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring; inclination refined
disk_measurements.notes: ALMA Cycle 4 higher-resolution follow-up; refines inclination to 65.6 +/- 0.4 deg; canonical geometry source for the main cold ring

disk_measurements.morphology: broad drawn-out inner disk extending ~10-90 AU; not a narrow asteroid-belt analog. PR-drag-fed continuous emission rather than a discrete warm ring.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI 15.5 and 23 um imaging revises the pre-JWST SED-inferred 8-15 AU / ~150 K narrow warm component (Su 2013) into a broad ~10-90 AU inner disk. Inclination 67.52 deg and PA 336.28 deg adopted by Gaspar 2023 for deprojection of the full inner system; consistent with White 2017 main-ring inclination 65.6 +/- 0.4 deg within uncertainties. Values are radial-profile extents; no formal fit-parameter error bars in the paper.

disk_measurements.morphology: eccentric ring-shaped brightness feature ~60-110 AU, e~0.3, misaligned relative to the outer cold ring. Inner gap demarcating the belt located at ~78 AU. Likely shepherded by an unseen planet.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI resolves a previously unknown eccentric intermediate belt at ~60-110 AU (paper-precise extents, replacing earlier shorthand of 40-80 AU). Eccentricity e~0.3 and misalignment vs. main cold ring argue for resonant sculpting by an unseen intermediate planet. Inner gap (~78 AU) separates this belt from the broad inner disk inward of it.

disk_measurements.morphology: main cold ring, resolved at 15.5 and 23 um
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI mid-IR imaging of the full multi-belt system; geometry parameters of the main ring not re-derived since ALMA (White 2017) is the canonical mm geometry source. Gaspar 2023 inclination 67.52 deg adopted for deprojection consistent with White 2017's 65.6 +/- 0.4 deg.

disk_measurements.morphology: extrasolar Kuiper-belt analog; cold component SED fit
disk_measurements.notes: Herschel/PACS far-IR imaging of the cold main ring; SED fit yields T_dust ~ 65 K; canonical dust-temperature source for the cold ring

disk_measurements.morphology: mm-cm grain population of the main cold ring
disk_measurements.notes: SONS legacy survey submm photometry; main-ring dust mass ~1.5e-2 M_Earth in mm-cm grains; lower-bound imaged-grain mass (collisional cascade modeling implies 4-10 M_Earth in planetesimal reservoir)

disk_measurements.morphology: IRAS 60 and 100 micron photometric excess; first 'Vega-like' debris disk detection
disk_measurements.notes: Discovery paper; no spatial resolution, geometry fields null

disk_measurements.morphology: first sub-mm resolved image of Vega's cold debris ring
disk_measurements.notes: 850 micron imaging; established cold ring structure but radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: OVRO 1.3 mm interferometric imaging; early sub-mm structure mapping of cold belt
disk_measurements.notes: Wilner, Holman, Kuchner & Ho 2002, 'Millimeter-Wave Aperture Synthesis Imaging of Vega: Evidence for a Ring Arc at 95 AU'; bibcode added 2026-05-29 audit (was null); specific radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: warm asteroid-analog inner belt at 14 AU; two-component SED + Spitzer-MIPS imaging
disk_measurements.notes: Canonical warm-belt fit (Spitzer-IRS + Herschel-PACS two-component SED)

disk_measurements.morphology: cold Kuiper-analog outer belt with inner edge at ~110 AU (audit 2026-05-29: was 62 AU; Su 2013 reports ~110 AU); wide cleared gap from the 14 AU warm belt implies planetesimal-stirring planets
disk_measurements.notes: Canonical cold-belt inner edge (two-component SED fit); outer edge taken from Sibthorpe 2010

disk_measurements.morphology: Herschel-PACS resolved imaging; cold belt extends to ~200 AU with relatively sharp outer edge, sharper inner edge than outer
disk_measurements.notes: 70/100/160 micron imaging; 70 AU inner-edge value (Su 2013 prefers 62 AU); outer-edge 200 AU is canonical for outer_radius_au

disk_measurements.morphology: SMA/early-ALMA 880 micron observations confirm cold-belt structure and constrain inner edge
disk_measurements.notes: Confirmation paper; specific radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: edge-on debris disk, first HST/ACS coronagraphic imaging; inner edge ~17–43 AU (broad), outer extension > 200 AU
edge-on debris disk traced from ~10 AU (cleared inner zone) to ~210 AU halo; surface-brightness break at ~35 AU (audit 2026-05-29: inner edge corrected 35 -> 10 AU)
disk_measurements.notes: Canonical geometry adopted for cfg synthesis (inner ~10 AU cleared, break ~35 AU, outer ~210 AU). Deep HST/STIS revisit refining Krist 2005.

disk_measurements.morphology: five fast-moving south-east-ansa density features, radial outflow 4–10 km/s (faster than Keplerian)
disk_measurements.notes: First detection of moving substructures (SPHERE polarimetric imaging). Inclination quoted to within ~1° of edge-on.

disk_measurements.morphology: multi-epoch confirmation of moving substructures; opacity ratio consistent with mid-range disk visibility
disk_measurements.notes: Two-year SPHERE monitoring; sets velocity precision and the wind-launched (not Keplerian) interpretation. Adopted for cfg disk_opacity = 0.4 tie-break.

disk_measurements.morphology: cold Spitzer IRS SED component (IRAS-discovered disk follow-up)
disk_measurements.notes: Cold-component dust temperature anchor (~50 K).

disk_measurements.morphology: birth-ring planetesimal belt at r_BR ~ 43 AU supplying dust to outer halo via radiation/wind pressure (audit 2026-05-29: was 35 AU; paper states 43 AU)
disk_measurements.notes: Theoretical birth-ring SED model, r_BR = 43 AU. Dust temperature 50 K removed (audit 2026-05-29: not in this paper; 50 K is from Chen 2005).

disk_measurements.morphology: warm asteroid-belt analog at ~1 AU; olivine-rich crystalline silicate grains (9.7, 11.3, 23 um features); sub-um grain population; no cold (70 um) excess
disk_measurements.notes: Anchor SED fit (Beichman 2005). L_IR/L_star = 2e-4 fractional luminosity; black-body color temperature ~400 K (fit by eye) -> small grains at ~0.5-1 AU; SED-constrained range 0.7-1.2 AU, best-fit r_max <= 1 AU; no 70 um excess (no cold belt). Dust mass 4.6e-9 M_earth = the observed sub-um (0.25 um) emitting-grain population (Beichman 2005: 2.7e19 g); the collisional-cascade total extrapolated to 10 km parent bodies is ~0.9e-3 M_earth (5.4e24 g, ~64x the solar asteroid belt, very uncertain) -- cf. the non-recommended Lisse 2007 entry. The 0.93 +/- 0.16 AU 'fit' and FWHM ~0.3 AU are Smith/Wyatt/Haniff 2009, not Beichman.

disk_measurements.morphology: HD 69830 unresolved by MIDI/VISIR; only a mid-IR interferometric upper limit on the dust size (< 2.4 AU). Does not independently constrain the warm-belt radii.
disk_measurements.notes: Audit 2026-05-29: prior entry mis-attributed the 0.92-1.16 AU SED range (actually Lisse 2007 / Beichman 2005) to this paper with method sed_fit. Smith, Wyatt & Haniff 2009 is interferometric and reports only a 0.05-2.4 AU unresolved size limit -- it does not provide a fitted belt geometry. Demoted from recommended; recommended geometry moved to Beichman 2005.

disk_measurements.morphology: post-collisional debris from a recent (<= 1 Myr) catastrophic disruption of a dwarf-planet-class body; thermally processed sub-um crystalline olivine dominant; planet d's 2:1 and 5:2 outer MMRs at 1.00 and 1.16 AU coincide with the belt and identify d as the dynamical sculptor
disk_measurements.notes: SED-fit grain composition model applied to Beichman 2005 IRS spectrum. Dust mass 1e-3 M_earth is the implied parent-body mass under the catastrophic-event interpretation, not a direct dust mass measurement. Identifies d's 2:1 + 5:2 outer mean-motion resonances at 1.00 / 1.16 AU as the belt's dynamical sculptors.

disk_measurements.morphology: observed L_IR/L_star ~ 2e-4 exceeds steady-state collisional cascade limit by ~10x; requires either recent stochastic event (favoring Lisse 2007 catastrophic interpretation) or an exceptionally massive parent reservoir
disk_measurements.notes: Theoretical steady-state collisional-cascade limit interpretation, not a direct disk measurement. Included as context for the catastrophic-vs-steady-state divergence preserved in the Phase 3 synthesis.

disk_measurements.morphology: single cold belt, KBO analog
disk_measurements.notes: 70/100/160 um resolved; dust mass quoted as 6-8x Sun's Edgeworth-Kuiper inventory, midpoint adopted via 5e-3 Mearth KBO reference

disk_measurements.morphology: broad single ring; no resolved inner gap
submm excess detection; KBO analog
two-component SED fit; superseded by MacGregor 2016 resolved imaging
